content: Vacation usage bar conventions in HR tools: (1) Table stakes: bar must measure the same thing as the number shown next to it. (2) Common approach: (used + planned) / total — this matches the 'remaining' number that subtracts both. (3) Two-segment bar (used solid + planned lighter/hatched) is the more informative pattern; single-segment (used+planned combined) is simpler. (4) Overdraft (negative remaining): do NOT clamp bar at 100%. Options: bar overflows with warning color; OR bar is full+warning color + a separate 'exceeded' number. Dayforce shows an 'Exceeded' column for negative balances. (5) Color convention: neutral/green when healthy, amber when low (e.g. ≤3 days), red when overdraft. (6) Pending approval vs confirmed planned: some tools distinguish (dotted/lighter segment for pending). Sources: Dayforce help, WhenIWork help, UX case study.
